description.en-US: Five disputed questions on the virtues in general, on charity, on fraternal correction, on hope, and on the cardinal virtues — held at Paris during Aquinas's second regency.
description.la: Quinque quaestiones disputatae de virtutibus in communi, de caritate, de correctione fraterna, de spe, et de virtutibus cardinalibus — apud Parisios in regentia secunda Thomae habitae.

sources.description: Latin text from the Leonine / Marietti editions (public domain), mirrored from aquinas.cc.

title.en-US: Question 1 — On the virtues in general
Prologue — On the virtues in general
Article 1 — Whether the virtues are habits
Article 2 — Whether the definition of virtue given by Augustine is fitting
Article 3 — Whether a power of the soul can be the subject of a virtue
Article 4 — Whether the irascible and concupiscible powers can be the subject of virtue
Article 5 — Whether the will is the subject of virtue
Article 6 — Whether there is virtue in the practical intellect as in a subject
Article 7 — Whether there is virtue in the speculative intellect
Article 8 — Whether virtues are in us by nature
Article 9 — Whether the virtues are acquired through actions
Article 10 — Whether there are any virtues in people through infusion
Article 11 — Whether infused virtue can be increased
Article 12 — The distinction of the virtues
Article 13 — Whether virtue consists in a mean
Question 2 — On charity
Prologue — On charity
Article 1 — Whether charity is something created in the soul or is itself the Holy Spirit
Article 2 — Whether charity is a virtue
Article 3 — Whether charity is the form of the virtues
Article 4 — Whether charity is one virtue
Article 5 — Whether or not charity is a specific virtue distinct from the other virtues
Article 6 — Whether charity can coexist with mortal sin
Article 7 — Whether the object of charity’s love is a rational nature
Article 8 — Whether loving one’s enemies belongs to the perfection of counsel
Article 9 — Whether there is an order to charity
Article 10 — Whether it is possible for charity to be perfected in this life
Article 11 — Whether everyone is required to have perfect charity
Article 12 — Whether charity, once possessed, can be lost
Article 13 — Whether charity is lost through a single act of mortal sin
Question 3 — On fraternal correction
Prologue — On fraternal correction
Article 1 — Whether fraternal correction is in a precept
Article 2 — Whether the order of fraternal correction is in the precept given in Matthew 18
Question 4 — On hope
Prologue — On hope
Article 1 — Whether hope is a virtue
Article 2 — Whether hope is in the will as its subject
Article 3 — Whether hope is prior to charity
Article 4 — Whether hope is only present in this life
Question 5 — On the cardinal virtues
Prologue — On the cardinal virtues
Article 1 — Whether the following are the cardinal virtues: justice, prudence, fortitude, and temperance
Article 2 — Whether the virtues are interconnected
Article 3 — Whether all the virtues in a person are equal
Article 4 — Whether the cardinal virtues will remain in the life to come
